spec_match.rationale: The page now creates real WebGL contexts with failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat, inspects renderer strings, and branches on null, software-renderer, and context-loss outcomes.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised scenario, context, fallback, caveat, and workload controls plus both action buttons, and each branch updated the rendered output, cards, and probe log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now matches the Chrome 139 behavior change: failed WebGL initialization no longer gets silent SwiftShader and app code must choose a fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers live WebGL, forced getContext null, forced software renderer, experimental-webgl, SVG/message/Canvas 2D fallbacks, and manual plus scenario-driven context loss.

references.rationale: The see-also section cites the required ChromeStatus URL, the Chromium SwiftShader deprecation note, and the WebGL context creation spec section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables for UI and Canvas 2D colors, has no source inline style attributes, and builds dynamic output with textContent and DOM nodes.

summary: The major fallback-path critique is closed. DevTools MCP verified live WebGL2, forced null with Canvas 2D, forced null with SVG, forced software with message fallback, experimental-webgl without the caveat flag, scenario-driven context loss, and manual context loss. Console output was clean and all page requests returned 200/204.
